format_discipline.meta_sidecar: present and valid (word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections)
format_discipline.sections: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways — References correctly omitted (zero citations, narrative_seed_only)

length.note: At the top edge of the standard band; prose count (sidecar 1599) is in range — the 1681 raw figure includes table markup, alt text, and headings. Watch on revision: any added prose tips it over.

citations.note: resource_count=0, citations_used=[], References omitted — correct for narrative_seed_only mode.

forward_references.note: Only prior vocab used: decomposition (1.5), abstraction (1.6), pseudocode/IF-OTHERWISE/REPEAT (1.7). No algorithm properties (1.9+), no code syntax. 'Bug' avoided — dead end glossed inline as 'a mistake in your logic'. Excalidraw introduced with a plain gloss.

faithfulness.note: Five-shape table, road-signs analogy, branch/loop/tracing definitions, vs-pseudocode table, 8-step drawing method, In Practice items, and Key Takeaways all trace to corpus. Worked Example trace (Start → Switch kettle on → Water boiled? → Wait 30s loop → Pour → Serve → End) verified against diagram.mmd node-for-node.

beginner_readability.note: Every term bold-defined at first use (flowchart, branch, tracing, dead end); five shape names introduced via table per formatting standards, each with plain meaning. Concrete-first throughout (tea chart, milk-in-fridge, road signs). No hedging, short sentences, 'you' register.

formatting.note: No wall-of-text (max ~3-sentence paragraphs). Procedures numbered (reading method, 8-step drawing method). Inline examples inside Key Concepts, not deferred. Comparison tables for shapes, vs-pseudocode, and Do/Don't. Why-does-this-matter posed-and-answered inline.

assessment_reference.note: Grep for assessment IDs, due weeks, grade weights: zero hits.

scope.note: Stays on the headline (flowchart shapes, reading, drawing). No algorithm properties, no code, no tool-depth beyond a one-line Excalidraw pointer. Abstraction reuse ('collapse a sub-task into one rectangle') is prior-topic vocab, not new teaching.

verdict: PASS. All hard and soft checks clear with zero findings. Clean H1 (no frontmatter), exact six-section template with References correctly omitted under narrative_seed_only (zero citations). Length in band at the upper edge (sidecar 1599 words / 1200-1600; ~7.0 min at 230 wpm vs 6-8 target). Diagram embedded inline in Key Concepts (line 25), ./diagram.png exists, sidecar embeds_diagram=true, and the Worked Example trace matches the rendered chart node-for-node. No forward references beyond 1.1-1.7 vocab, no assessment IDs/weeks/weights in prose, 11/11 sampled assertions corpus-supported. Meets the gold bar set by 2-1-what-makes-a-good-specification (define-then-example structure, inline good/bad concreteness, beginner register); notably it avoids the assessment-reference defect flagged in gold notes.
